phrases.text_local: Scusi, dove inizia il portico San Luca, per favore?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, DOH-veh ee-NEE-tsa eel POR-tee-koh san LOO-kah, per fah-VOH-reh

responses.english: From Porta Saragozza, at the church.

phrases.text_local: Scusi, c'è un autobus per San Luca, per favore?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee cheh oon OW-toh-boos pehr sahn LOO-kah pehr fah-VOH-reh

responses.phonetic: SÌ LAH lee-NEH-ah trehn-TAHT-ré PAHR-teh DAH KW-ee
responses.french: Sì, la linea trentatré parte da qui.

phrases.text_local: Scusi, serve un biglietto per il portico, per favore?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, SER-veh oon bee-LYET-toh per eel POR-tee-koh, per fah-VOH-reh

phrases.intent_en: Ask how many arches the San Luca portico has
phrases.text_local: Scusi, quanti archi ha il portico di San Luca?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, KWAN-tee AR-kee ah eel por-TEE-koh dee san LOO-kah?
phrases.cultural_note: The San Luca portico, with its 666 arches, is the world's longest, running 3.8 km up to the Sanctuary of the Madonna di San Luca.

responses.english: Six hundred and sixty-six arches, the longest in the world.
responses.phonetic: seh-een-toh-ses-san-tah-SEH-ee AR-kee, eel PYOO LOON-goh del MON-doh.
responses.french: Seicentosessantasei archi, il più lungo del mondo.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the walk is suitable for children
phrases.text_local: Scusi, la camminata al portico di San Luca è adatta ai bambini?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, lah kam-mee-NAH-tah al por-TEE-koh dee san LOO-kah eh ah-DAT-tah ay bam-BEE-nee?

responses.english: Yes, but it's quite steep — better with a sturdy pushchair.
responses.phonetic: see, mah eh PYOO-tos-toh ree-PEE-dah — MEL-lyoh kon pas-sej-JEE-noh roh-BOOS-toh.
responses.french: Sì, ma è piuttosto ripida — meglio con passeggino robusto.
